meta.groupingHelp: Edit topics[] for section titles and order. Each use case has topicId (must match a topic id) and order (sort key within that section, lower first).

useCases.title: I want to download the HITT script
notes: Optional: curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mwaltersbmc/helix-tools/main/hitt/dbjars.tgz in the same directory to enable database validation (see README).
Run as the git user on the Deployment Engine where Jenkins is installed.

useCases.title: I want to change my HITT configuration
commands: Or delete the file and re-run HITT to be prompted again:
notes: hitt.conf holds HP_NAMESPACE, IS_NAMESPACE, IS_CUSTOMER_SERVICE, IS_ENVIRONMENT, Jenkins host/credentials, and tool paths.
Deleting hitt.conf forces the interactive wizard on next run (cluster must be reachable for namespace discovery unless you use overrides).
For a second config file without renaming the default, keep multiple files and pass -c /path/to/other.conf when you run HITT.

useCases.title: I want to run the standard health checks (post-HP / pre-IS / post-IS / Jenkins)
notes: Requires hitt.conf (namespaces, CUSTOMER_SERVICE, ENVIRONMENT, Jenkins URL if applicable).
post-hp and pre-hp skip Jenkins checks; see hitt/README.md for when to use each mode.

useCases.title: I want to use a different hitt.conf file
notes: Combine -c with any mode or feature that normally reads hitt.conf.

useCases.title: I want to show the contents of a Kubernetes secret
notes: First argument is the secret name, second is the namespace (matches kubectl -n).
Printable keys are shown as text; other keys are decoded to files in the current directory (see decodeK8sSecret in hitt.sh).

useCases.title: I want the live IS database ID (DBID) from the cluster
notes: Uses hitt.conf, cluster access, and IS REST to read the current DBID (licensing).

useCases.title: I want an AR-JWT for the IS REST API
notes: Default user is hannah_admin with password resolved from the cluster when no user is given.
With a username only, password is prompted if not passed as a second argument.

useCases.title: I want to generate a DBID string before deployment (from DB type and names)
notes: DB_TYPE is one of: mssql, oracle, postgres.
This does not call the cluster; it only computes the ID from the three values.

useCases.title: I want a quick list of utility commands
notes: Same content as README-utility-mode.md summary lines.

useCases.title: I want to check HELIX_ONPREM_DEPLOYMENT pipeline values before a deployment
notes: Outputs JSON to the console unless you pass a filename as the last argument.
Requires Jenkins in hitt.conf and working credentials.
Useful to compare defaults vs last successful build before changing inputs.

useCases.title: I want to push saved pipeline values into Jenkins as a new parameter build
notes: After this, open HELIX_ONPREM_DEPLOYMENT in Jenkins, rebuild the last job, and review parameters (README warns the generated build is expected to fail until you adjust values).
PRODUCT sub-pipelines are forced off so a full deploy does not start from this step alone.

useCases.title: I want the latest Jenkins console log for a pipeline
notes: PIPELINE_NAME is the Jenkins job name as shown in the URL (underscores).

useCases.title: I want to display Jenkins credential usernames/passwords (sensitive)
notes: Also writes kubeconfig from Jenkins to kubeconfig.jenkins in the current directory when applicable.
Use only on a trusted machine; rotate secrets if this output is ever leaked.

useCases.title: I want to update the Jenkins kubeconfig credential with a new kubeconfig file
notes: Without a path, HITT uses ~/.kube/config. The file is validated before updating the Jenkins credential.
When Jenkins runs in-cluster, only certain jenkins fix sub-modes are allowed; see script messages if you hit that guard.

useCases.title: I want to refresh Jenkins username/password and related credentials (except kubeconfig)
notes: You will be prompted for the git user password where required.
Does not update the kubeconfig credential; use jenkins kubeconfig separately.

useCases.title: I want to run all Jenkins-oriented fixes at once (new Jenkins host)
notes: Runs the Jenkins fix bundle except dryrun. Review README-fix-mode for what each piece does.

useCases.title: I want to replace the Helix IS cacerts secret with a new truststore file
notes: You are prompted to confirm when the new file validates.

useCases.title: I want to apply an AR / Innovation Suite license on the running system
notes: Optional expiry uses DD-Mon-YY format when required for temporary keys.

useCases.title: I want the list of fix modes from the script
notes: Prints the same categories as README-fix-mode.md tables.

useCases.title: I want a tctl client config snippet (RSSO URL, client id/secret, app URL) from the cluster
notes: Special case: does not deploy the tctl job/pod. Reads TMS/RSSO secrets and deployment data from the Helix Platform namespace and prints YAML fields (appurl, clientid, clientsecret, enableauth, rssourl).
TMS credentials are also written to the terminal (/dev/tty) when available.
Requires hitt.conf and a full Helix Platform deployment (blocked on CORE-only / no tenant services).

useCases.title: I want to run a simple tctl command without installing tctl locally
notes: Deploys the same style job/pod used by HELIX_ITSM_INTEROPS; output prints when the job completes.
Not valid for Helix Platform CORE-only deployments (script will error with the documented message).
For a static tctl client YAML snippet from the cluster (no job), use bash hitt.sh -t "config" instead.

useCases.title: I want IS bundle deployment status from a bundle ID
notes: ID is the value from the pipeline STATUS URI (see README example).

useCases.title: I want shell trace (xtrace) while HITT runs
notes: With default logging (no -l), HITT enables set -x before running main and still tees output to hitt.log.
If you pass -l to disable log files, the script currently runs main without the tee branch, so -d does not enable set -x in that combination—omit -l when you need xtrace.

useCases.title: I want to see the long help text for a specific HITT message ID
notes: Use the numeric ID shown in parentheses after ERROR or WARNING, e.g. (127).
The command must be exactly two script arguments after the shell name (e.g. hitt.sh -e 127) so the built-in lookup runs and then exits.

useCases.title: I want trace plus stop early for troubleshooting (debug + stop on first matching condition)
notes: -e 0 exits the script the first time stopOnError runs (any logged ERROR or WARNING that invokes it), which is useful to freeze right after the first failure while -d shows the command trace leading up to it.
You can instead stop on a specific ID: bash hitt.sh -d -e 127 -m post-is
